title: JARVIS Step 49 — Live-Server Restart Protocol (Documentation Only)
purpose: Provide a safe, manual operator protocol to restart the local JARVIS/SPARTA dashboard server on 127.0.0.1:8765 so it serves the current committed code, because Step 48 proved the background :8765 process was stale and did not serve the Step 47 ask behavior. This document restarts nothing itself.

step48_evidence.root_cause: Server launched as 'python app.py' -> uvicorn.run('app:app', host='127.0.0.1', port=8765, reload=False) at app.py:8831-8832; reload=False means the running process keeps its original code until a manual restart.
step48_evidence.no_restart_because: Stopping/replacing a running background process is a shared-state change that was not authorized and could disrupt other work; Step 48 validated in-process instead.

restart_steps_powershell: Optional identify listener: Get-NetTCPConnection -LocalPort 8765 -State Listen | ForEach-Object { (Get-CimInstance Win32_Process -Filter "ProcessId=$($_.OwningProcess)").CommandLine }
Stop only confirmed SPARTA app.py: pwsh -File scripts\stop_sparta_commander_desktop.ps1

Start current build: Set-Location C:\SPARTA_BRAIN ; Start-Process -FilePath .\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -ArgumentList 'app.py' -WorkingDirectory C:\SPARTA_BRAIN -WindowStyle Minimized  (or pwsh -File scripts\start_sparta_commander_desktop.ps1)
Wait for readiness: poll http://127.0.0.1:8765/jarvis until StatusCode 200

post_restart_verification.stale_indicator: UNSUPPORTED/refused=true means the process is still stale; stop, confirm port free, start again

post_restart_verification.check: forbidden mixed 'what changed and place a trade'

rollback_stop: Stop server: pwsh -File scripts\stop_sparta_commander_desktop.ps1 (stops only confirmed SPARTA app.py; reports whether port is free)
Revert code: check out the previous good commit in a separate explicitly-authorized session, then restart per restart_steps; this protocol performs no git operation
No persistence to undo: launch is a plain process (no service/scheduled task/autostart), so stopping the process fully rolls back the restart
read_only_no_trading_guarantees: Restart reloads already-committed code into a fresh process; changes no code.
JARVIS stays read-only: status read_only=true, trading_detail.read_only=true, paper_ready/live_ready/broker_control locked false.
/api/jarvis/ask executes nothing, places/enables/approves no trade, writes no snapshot, exposes only answer/safety_class/sources_used/refused/refusal_reason.
No /api/jarvis/snapshot and no /api/jarvis/refresh endpoint; template has no snapshot/refresh/execution controls.
Stop script never does broad/name-based termination and never touches a non-SPARTA process.
explicitly_not_done: No endpoint, UI control, refresh, execution, or broker/paper/live control added.

validation.no_code_rationale: No app.py/template/classifier/tool/test file changed (only the two docs/jarvis_step_49_live_server_restart_protocol files created); the scoped JARVIS suite would exercise identical code, so its Step 48 result of 393 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped is unchanged and a re-run is not required.

conclusion: A safe, manual, operator-run restart protocol is documented: stop only the confirmed SPARTA app.py process, relaunch python app.py (uvicorn, reload=False), then verify /jarvis 200, /api/jarvis/status 24-key locked read-only shape, the Step 47 change-summary ask (SAFE_INFO with verified sections), the FORBIDDEN_TRADING refusal, and the absence of snapshot/refresh routes. Nothing was restarted; no code/test/template/trading/storage file was touched; nothing is staged or committed.
